task.title: Audit read model wording in README skills and tools
task.summary: Align public package language so generated read models are called views and source-of-truth files are called canonical truth.

goal.outcome: User-facing and agent-facing text use the same v2 vocabulary for views and canonical truth.
acceptance: README describes views and the canonical/view boundary.
Skills tell agents to consume views and avoid editing views.
Tool descriptions mention rebuilding views where relevant.

delta.desired: Public and agent instructions use view vocabulary consistently.
delta.current: Some package text still says projection, derived state, or generated state in places where v2 wants view terminology.
delta.closure: README, skills, and tool descriptions match the lexicon.

acceptance_matrix.criterion: README describes views and the canonical/view boundary.
Skills tell agents to consume views and avoid editing views.
Tool descriptions mention rebuilding views where relevant.

recent_evidence.title: Audit read model wording in README skills and tools
recent_evidence.summary: Audited and aligned read model wording across README, skills, and tools. Replaced "projection", "derived state", "generated state", and "read model" with "view", and "source of truth" with "canonical truth".
